---
title: "igraph sfnetwork"
format: html
editor: visual
---

# Spatial networks/graphs - introduction

## Goals for this script

- Creating a nework/graph from spatial data  
- Basic operations with graphs  
- Spatial nework visualization  
- Packages [igraph](https://r.igraph.org/articles/igraph.html) and [sfnetwork](https://luukvdmeer.github.io/sfnetworks/)
- **Build a simple network, find and visualize shortest path between two nodes**

```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

library(sf)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(sfnetworks)
library(tidygraph)
library(igraph)
library(mapview)
library(knitr)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Build a simplified cycleway network

```{r}
roxel <- sfnetworks::roxel |>
  filter(type == "cycleway")

net <- roxel |>
  as_sfnetwork(
    directed = FALSE,
    length_as_weight = TRUE,
    edges_as_lines = TRUE
  ) |>
  convert(to_spatial_smooth) |>
  mutate(component = group_components(type = "weak")) |>
  filter(component == 1) |>
  activate("nodes") |>
  mutate(
    node_id = row_number(),
    vertex_name = paste0("v", node_id)
  ) |>
  activate("edges") |>
  mutate(
    edge_id = row_number(),
    dist_m = as.numeric(st_length(geometry)),
    label = paste0(round(dist_m), " m")
  )

edges <- net |>
  activate("edges") |>
  st_as_sf() |>
  select(edge_id, name, type, dist_m, geometry) |>
  mutate(
    dist_m = as.numeric(dist_m),
    name = as.character(name),
    type = as.character(type)
  )

nodes <- net |>
  activate("nodes") |>
  st_as_sf()

roxel # orignial sf object with linestring geometries
net # simple network, created using: roxel |> as_sfnetwork()
edges # edges of the network, created using: net |> activate("edges")
nodes # nodes of the network, created using: net |> activate("nodes")
```


------------------------------------------------------------------------


## Display the network (`ggplot2` and `mapview`)

```{r}
#| warning: FALSE
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 6


# ggplot2
ggplot() +
  geom_sf(
    data = edges,
    aes(color = dist_m, linewidth = dist_m),
    alpha = 0.85
  ) +
  geom_sf(
    data = nodes,
    color = "red",
    size = 2,
    alpha = 0.8
  ) +
  geom_sf_text(
    data = nodes,
    aes(label = vertex_name),
    size = 2.5,
    check_overlap = TRUE,
    nudge_y = 0.00003
  ) +
  scale_linewidth_continuous(range = c(0.3, 1.8)) +
  labs(
    title = "Spatial graph from sfnetworks::roxel",
    subtitle = "Cycleways in Roxel, Münster, Germany",
    color = "Edge length (m)",
    linewidth = "Edge length (m)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title.position = "plot",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "right"
  )
# mapview 
mapview(edges, zcol = "dist_m", lwd = 3, layer.name = "Edge (m)") +
  mapview(nodes, col.regions = "red", cex = 5, layer.name = NULL)

```


------------------------------------------------------------------------


##  Distance matrix

* Matrix of shortest-path distances along the network

```{r}
g <- igraph::as.igraph(net)

# Edge weights
w <- igraph::edge_attr(g, "dist_m")

# All-pairs shortest-path distance matrix
D <- igraph::distances(
  g,
  weights = w
)

# Use vertex names as row and column names
rownames(D) <- igraph::vertex_attr(g, "vertex_name")
colnames(D) <- igraph::vertex_attr(g, "vertex_name")

knitr::kable(
  round(D, 0),
  caption = "Shortest-path distance matrix among selected vertices, in metres"
)
```


------------------------------------------------------------------------


##  Display shortest path for two selected vertices


```{r}
#| warning: FALSE
from_vertex <- "v14"
to_vertex   <- "v4"

from_id <- match(from_vertex, igraph::vertex_attr(g, "vertex_name"))
to_id   <- match(to_vertex, igraph::vertex_attr(g, "vertex_name"))

sp <- igraph::shortest_paths(
  g,
  from = from_id,
  to = to_id,
  weights = w,
  output = "both"
)

path_edge_ids <- igraph::edge_attr(g, "edge_id")[as.integer(sp$epath[[1]])]
path_node_ids <- igraph::vertex_attr(g, "node_id")[as.integer(sp$vpath[[1]])]

path_edges <- edges |>
  filter(edge_id %in% path_edge_ids)

path_nodes <- nodes |>
  filter(node_id %in% path_node_ids)

path_length <- sum(path_edges$dist_m, na.rm = TRUE)

path_length


# plot the shortest path with ggplot2
ggplot() +
  geom_sf(
    data = edges,
    color = "grey75",
    linewidth = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_sf(
    data = path_edges,
    color = "red",
    linewidth = 1.6
  ) +
  geom_sf(
    data = nodes,
    color = "grey30",
    size = 1.5,
    alpha = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_sf(
    data = path_nodes,
    color = "red",
    size = 3
  ) +
  geom_sf_text(
    data = path_nodes,
    aes(label = vertex_name),
    size = 3,
    nudge_y = 0.00004,
    check_overlap = TRUE
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Shortest path in the Roxel cycleway network",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "From ", from_vertex, " to ", to_vertex,
      "; path length = ", round(path_length, 1), " m"
    )
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )
# use mapview
mapview(edges, zcol = "dist_m", lwd = 3, layer.name = "Edge (m)") +
  mapview(nodes, col.regions = "grey50", cex = 5, layer.name = NULL) +
  mapview(path_edges, color = "red", lwd = 6, alpha =1, layer.name = "shortest path")
```
